user_message: Pulling in context from Coho Winery's email thread -- need to draft the follow-up for the partner engagement.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 10:09 EST
Subject: partner engagement - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with partner engagement. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-02-04 08:17 EST
Subject: Re: partner engagement Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about partner engagement. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-09 16:39 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Coho Winery partner engagement

Our team encountered some challenges with partner engagement. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Coho Winery: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF37-B trap (Over-escalates a routine service complaint); produces complete Severity triage, root-cause hypothesis (labeled as hypothesis), customer response draft, ACR risk fl
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF37-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF37-B failure mode (Over-escalates a routine service complaint)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF37-B -- Over-escalates a routine service complaint
